name: github-policy
description: GitHub repository management policy governing code push, branch management, and repository operations — Monorepo mode

policy.principle: All websites deployed to ONE monorepo (michaelbmt86-lang/websitefactory). Each domain gets its own folder. Code is pushed via Git Data API. Never create additional repositories.

authentication.description: GitHub token must have repo and webhook scopes

repository.description: Single monorepo for all website deployments. Never create additional repositories.

projectFolders.rule: If project folder already exists → update it. If not → create it. Never duplicate folders.

push.description: Code is pushed via GitHub Git Data API. push_code MUST only commit/update/push the current project folder. Never overwrite other project folders.

webhooks.description: Webhooks are created during deployment for CI/CD triggers

cleanup.description: Repository is never deleted during rollback — only deployment is reverted
